title: Oru Kili Urugudhu Song Lyrics Anandha Kummi
stanzas:
Oru Kili Urugudhu Song Lyrics Anandha Kummi
#Ilaiyaraja

Oru Kili Urugudhu
urimaiyil pazhagudhu
oh myna myna

kurumbugal thodarudhu
arumbugal valarudhu
oh myna myna

thalirudhu malarudhu thaana
idhu oru thodarkadhai thaanaa

iru manam inaiyudhu
iru kili thazhuvudhu

oh myna myna

oh myna myna

kilinjalgale ulairasi
ival allavaa ilavarasi

thaen aadum poovellaam
thazhpodum
oru kili madiyinil
oru kili urangudhu
oh myna myna

oh myna myna

vidhaikiradhe siru paravai

neerodai engengum

malargalin veligalil
irupirai valarudhu
oh myna oh myna

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song Oru Kili Urugudhu conveys themes of love and longing, using the metaphor of a bird to express deep emotions.

What is the plot context of the movie Anandha Kummi?

The movie revolves around themes of love and relationships, showcasing the emotional struggles of its characters.

Who is the music director of this song?

The song is composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja, known for his unique musical style.

When was the song released?

Oru Kili Urugudhu was released on January 6, 1983.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Oru Kili Urugudhu, the lyrics beautifully depict the feelings of love and nostalgia, resonating deeply with listeners. The song serves as a poignant reflection of the characters' emotional journeys in the movie Anandha Kummi. With its soothing melody, it evokes a sense of longing and tranquility. This classic piece remains a cherished part of Tamil music history.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Oru Kili Urugudhu from the Anandha Kummi movie is a timeless classic that beautifully captures the essence of nostalgia. With its soothing melody and evocative lyrics, this song transports listeners to a serene emotional landscape. Experience the magic of Ilaiyaraaja's composition that resonates with the heart and soul.
stanzaMeanings: A little bird is trembling in its rights, oh myna myna.
The young ones are growing, the buds are blooming, oh myna myna.
Is this a story of a sprout and a flower?
Two hearts are connected, two birds are embracing, oh myna myna.
A little bird is trembling in its rights, oh myna myna.
The young ones are growing, the buds are blooming, oh myna myna.
In the moonlight and in the nights, oh myna myna, the sand outside is shining, oh myna myna.
The little ones are flying, isn't she a princess?
All the flowers that sing honey, a little bird is sleeping in a nest, oh myna myna.
A little bird is trembling in its rights, oh myna myna.
The young ones are growing, the buds are blooming, oh myna myna.
Is this a story of a sprout and a flower?
Two hearts are connected, two birds are embracing, oh myna myna.
A little bird is trembling in its rights, oh myna myna.
In the fields and in the valleys, oh myna oh myna, two cuckoos are calling, oh myna oh myna.
In the fields, many dreams are being sown by the little birds.
The water flows everywhere like a flower stream.
In the rays of flowers, the beauty is blooming, oh myna oh myna.
A little bird is trembling in its rights, oh myna myna.
The young ones are growing, the buds are blooming, oh myna myna.
Is this a story of a sprout and a flower?
Two hearts are connected, two birds are embracing, oh myna myna.
